claim: Deep learning improved image classification accuracy.
evidence: Deep learning improved image classification accuracy on benchmark datasets compared with shallow baselines.

claim: Data augmentation reduces overfitting in image classifiers.
evidence: Data augmentation reduced overfitting in image classifiers by increasing the diversity of training images.

claim: Neural networks are used in medicine.
evidence: Neural networks have been applied to medical image classification tasks in radiology.
notes: Specific medical imaging evidence only partly supports broad medicine claim.

claim: Citation checking can help students revise academic drafts.
evidence: A citation checking system identified missing citations in student drafts and produced revision targets for instructors.
notes: Evidence supports a mechanism but not the full educational outcome.

claim: Large language models can assist academic writing.
evidence: Large language models can generate fluent summaries and draft text, but they may hallucinate unsupported facts.
notes: Mixed evidence: assistance is plausible but caveated.

evidence: Large language models can hallucinate unsupported facts and do not eliminate errors.

claim: Deep learning requires no data preprocessing.
evidence: Deep learning performance often depends on careful data preprocessing and representation choices.
notes: Evidence says preprocessing is often required.

claim: The proposed model increased classification accuracy.
evidence: The proposed model showed no significant improvement, and classification accuracy was lower than the baseline.

claim: Graph neural networks improve protein folding accuracy.

evidence: Dropout reduced overfitting in the evaluated neural networks by randomly suppressing activations during training.
